ccb87ebb-616c-415d-a81b-7911b2e9f3aa.text: Reference to "days" shall be construed as calendar days unless qualified by the word "business", in which instance a "business day" shall be any day other than a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday as gazetted by the government of the Republic of South Africa from time to time. Any reference to "business hours" shall be construed as being the hours between 08:00 and 16:30 on any business day.

3 DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

3.

7a98479f-8c69-4efc-9ec3-8f5cddaf2df0.label: text_chunk
7a98479f-8c69-4efc-9ec3-8f5cddaf2df0.text:
1 The Disclosing Party shall only disclose the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party to the extent deemed necessary or desirable by the Disclosing Party in its sole discretion.

3.2 The Receiving Party acknowledges that the Confidential  Information is a valuable,

 special and unique asset proprietary to the Disclosing Party.

3.3 The Receiving Party agrees that it will,

 during or after the course of their relationship and/or the term of this Agreement as described in clause 9,

d7955611-c2d5-4c63-9825-0f435331a87d.label: text_chunk
d7955611-c2d5-4c63-9825-0f435331a87d.text: keep the Confidential Information in the strictest confidence and will not disclose it to any third party for any reason or purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, save in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, and the Parties undertake to each other that their holding and subsidiary companies or agents shall be bound by the provisions of this Agreement.

663719f9-dfac-455b-a0ba-8c50aa611b94.label: text_chunk
663719f9-dfac-455b-a0ba-8c50aa611b94.text:
For avoidance of doubt, in this Agreement “third party” means any party other than Client and AfriGIS and their holding and subsidiary companies or agents.

3.

55a6aa42-5931-48a7-81c6-093bf472f702.label: text_chunk
55a6aa42-5931-48a7-81c6-093bf472f702.text: 4 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement the Parties agree that the Confidential Information may be disclosed by the Receiving Party to its professional advisors on a need-to-know basis; provided that that Party takes whatever steps are necessary to procure that such professional advisors agree to abide by the terms of this Agreement to prevent the unauthorised disclosure of the Confidential Information to third parties.

ebb6b07d-1526-4497-b8ca-e4c74002accb.label: text_chunk
ebb6b07d-1526-4497-b8ca-e4c74002accb.text:
For purposes of this clause, the Receiving Party’s professional advisers and employees, directors or managers shall be deemed to be acting, in the event of a breach, as that Party’s duly authorised agents.

3.5 The Receiving Party agrees –

3.5.1 not  to utilise,

 exploit or in any other manner whatsoever use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever whether for its own benefit or for that of others without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party;

3.5.

7
holds the Disclosing Party harmless against any loss, claim, harm or damage, of whatever  nature, suffered or sustained by the Disclosing Party pursuant to a breach by the Receiving Party of the provisions of this Agreement.

4.1 to be proprietary to the Disclosing Party; and

4.

2 not to confer any rights to the Receiving Party of whatever nature in the Confidential Information.

19bd292f-5825-4b75-a85d-745adaa51aad.label: text_chunk
19bd292f-5825-4b75-a85d-745adaa51aad.text:
5 RESTRICTIONS ON DISCLOSURE AND USE OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

The Receiving Party undertakes not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than –

5.1 that for which it is disclosed;

5.2 in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement; and

5.3 in accordance with applicable data privacy legislation, as amended from time to time.

e64e93ef-4205-465c-8a18-b32d7e71da9b.label: text_chunk
e64e93ef-4205-465c-8a18-b32d7e71da9b.text:
6 STANDARD OF CARE

The Receiving Party agrees that it shall protect the Confidential Information disclosed to or obtained by it by using the same standard of care that the Receiving Party applies to safeguard its own proprietary, secret or Confidential Information  and that the information shall be stored and handled in such a way as to prevent any unauthorised disclosure thereof.

7 RETURN OF MATERIAL CONTAINING OR PERTAINING TO THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

7.1 The Disclosing Party may,

c65d0479-b6f3-42de-b3f0-da3c4250cd68.label: text_chunk
c65d0479-b6f3-42de-b3f0-da3c4250cd68.text:
at any time,

 request the Receiving  Party to return any material containing, pertaining to or relating to the Confidential Information and may, in addition request the Receiving Party to furnish a written statement to the effect that, upon such return, the Receiving Party has not retained in its possession, or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such material.8

7.2 As an alternative to the return of the material contemplated in clause 7.

22d6f5b4-e167-4bf4-ae8c-bc2e64f6b882.label: text_chunk
22d6f5b4-e167-4bf4-ae8c-bc2e64f6b882.text:
1, the Receiving Party shall, at the instance of  the Disclosing Party, destroy such material and furnish the Disclosing Party with a written statement from authorised officers to the effect that all such material has been destroyed.

7.3 The Receiving Party shall comply with a request in terms of this clause,

 within 14 (fourteen) calendar days of receipt of such a request.

bf8e7b9a-936b-4b18-ba65-c9350567bcb3.label: text_chunk
bf8e7b9a-936b-4b18-ba65-c9350567bcb3.text:
8 EXCLUDED CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

The obligations of the Receiving Party pursuant to the provisions of this Agreement shall not apply to any Confidential Information that –

8.1 is or was known to,

 or in the possession of the Receiving Party prior to disclosure thereof by the Disclosing Party;

8.2 is or becomes publicly known,

 otherwise than as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party;

8.

f4d20bac-8ae5-448c-85b3-846dbdfae453.label: text_chunk

b8c7957a-0a81-4f3a-ba07-3a90775ded7f.label: text_chunk
b8c7957a-0a81-4f3a-ba07-3a90775ded7f.text:
the Receiving Party shall advise the Disclosing Party to take whatever steps it deems necessary to protect its interests in this regard and provided further that the Receiving Party will disclose only that portion of the information which it is legally required to disclose and the Receiving Party will use its reasonable endeavours to protect the confidentiality of such information to the greatest extent possible in the circumstances;

8.

65a4af10-df25-4e3a-835c-641ef52b257c.label: text_chunk
65a4af10-df25-4e3a-835c-641ef52b257c.text:

8.

6 is received from a third party in circumstances that do not result in a breach of the provisions of this Agreement.9

9 TERM

This Agreement and the undertakings contained herein shall commence upon the Signature Date and shall continue for a period of 2 (two) years thereafter.

a586a6b7-5a7a-4c11-a4f7-781db53e2dcd.label: text_chunk
a586a6b7-5a7a-4c11-a4f7-781db53e2dcd.text:
10 CO-OPERATION

The Parties undertake at all times to co-operate with each other in good faith in order to carry out this Agreement and to  implement all transactions and steps contemplated herein.

11 WARRANTIES BY PARTIES

Each Party warrants that –

11.

1 it has the legal capacity and has taken all necessary corporate action required to empower and authorise it to enter into and implement this Agreement on the terms and conditions herein set out;

11.

1bfc1ce9-bc73-4986-a7dc-a45f69f2b78e.label: text_chunk
1bfc1ce9-bc73-4986-a7dc-a45f69f2b78e.text:
2 this Agreement constitutes an agreement valid and binding on it and enforceable against it in accordance with its terms.

12 BREACH

In the event that a Party should breach the provisions of this Agreement, then the other Party shall be entitled to invoke all remedies available to it in law including without limitation the institution of urgent interim proceedings, an action for damages and/or specific performance and/or any other equitable relief for any threatened or actual breach of the provisions of this Agreement.

13 DISPUTES

13.

372227e6-60cb-4882-a303-e0d36fd2b1c0.label: text_chunk
372227e6-60cb-4882-a303-e0d36fd2b1c0.text: 1 In the event of any dispute or difference arising between the Parties relating to or arising out of this Agreement, including the implementation, execution, interpretation, rectification,  termination or cancellation of this Agreement, the chief executive officers of the Parties  or any of their designated officials shall upon request by any Party meet to attempt to settle such dispute or difference, and failing settlement within a period of  7 (seven) business days from such a request,

004b7bf5-4999-4137-bad3-7e7a5f977e6d.label: text_chunk
004b7bf5-4999-4137-bad3-7e7a5f977e6d.text: the said dispute or difference shall on demand by any Party  be submitted to arbitration in Johannesburg in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Foundation of Southern Africa by an arbitrator or arbitrators appointed by the Foundation.

ddd6ec9a-9019-4665-8604-cd83d888c17b.label: text_chunk
ddd6ec9a-9019-4665-8604-cd83d888c17b.text:

13.2.1 will be binding on all of them;

13.2.2 will forthwith be carried into effect;

13.2.3 may be made an order of any court of competent jurisdiction.

13.

3 Nothing herein contained shall be deemed to prevent or prohibit either Party from applying to the appropriate court for urgent relief.

13.

b1e1ea0e-80be-4041-a7ba-289067b8ac75.label: text_chunk
b1e1ea0e-80be-4041-a7ba-289067b8ac75.text:

14 STIPULATIO ALTERI

To the extent that any provisions of this Agreement purport to be for the benefit of a Client  Brand, the Parties confirm that such provisions are intended to be a stipulatio alteri (i.e. a contract for the benefit of a third party) capable of acceptance at any time by such Client Brand.

724c095f-9d8b-4f2d-89af-0b5235879aa4.label: text_chunk
724c095f-9d8b-4f2d-89af-0b5235879aa4.text:
Such acceptance may only take place by way of a written notice to that effect given to AfriGIS by Client on behalf of the Client Brand in question.

15 NON-SOLICITATION

Unless agreed to the contrary between the parties each Party hereby undertakes in favour of the other that it will not at any time during the currency of this Agreement and for a period of 12 months after the expiry or termination of this Agreement (for any reason whatsoever), whether directly or indirectly:

15.

1 encourage or entice or incite or persuade any employee of the other (or any employee of any member of each other's group of companies or any entity in which either is interested) to terminate their employment by the other of them (or any member of its group of companies or any entity in which either is interested); and/or

15.2.

17 APPLICABLE LAW AND JURISDICTION

17.

1 This Agreement will in all respects be governed by and construed under the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

eeb4f814-10d0-4447-a25d-3169911ecca9.label: text_chunk
eeb4f814-10d0-4447-a25d-3169911ecca9.text:
17.2 Subject to the provisions of this Agreement,

 the Parties hereto hereby consent and submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the North Gauteng Provincial Division of the High Court of the Republic of South Africa in any dispute arising from or in connection with this Agreement. The Parties agree that any costs awarded  will be recoverable in accordance with the High Court tariff, determined on an attorney-and-own-client scale.

5e875710-4ecb-425d-a987-ac89eb8282f6.label: text_chunk
5e875710-4ecb-425d-a987-ac89eb8282f6.text:
18 SEVERABILITY

If any clause or term of this Agreement or part thereof should be invalid, unenforceable, defective or illegal for any reason whatsoever, then the remaining terms and provisions of this Agreement shall be deemed to be severable therefrom and shall continue in full force and effect unless such invalidity, unenforceability, defect or illegality goes to the root of this Agreement.

576eeb62-5413-44e9-82ff-30c15347d24d.label: text_chunk
576eeb62-5413-44e9-82ff-30c15347d24d.text:
19 COUNTERPARTS AND ELECTRONIC SIGNATURE

19.1 This Agreement may be signed in one or more counterparts,

 each of which shall constitute an original and all of which shall collectively constitute one and the same agreement.

19.

2 This Agreement shall be validly signed notwithstanding that the signature page is signed by the Parties in different locations and returned by email.

44d74aae-0200-414b-89eb-8f3ff938322f.label: text_chunk
44d74aae-0200-414b-89eb-8f3ff938322f.text:
In these circumstances the Parties shall endeavour to circulate the original Agreement for signature, but the failure to do so  shall not invalidate this Agreement or change the date of its signature or coming into force.13

20 COSTS

Each Party shall bear its own costs of and in connection with the negotiation, drafting and preparation of this Agreement.

21.

 promise or the like, not recorded herein.

21.5 The Parties undertake at all times to do all such things,

community_summary.0:
The relationships involving AfriGIS and its clients are centered around formal contractual agreements, specifically confidentiality and non-disclosure agreements. These agreements establish a business relationship where AfriGIS provides services to its clients. 

Key entities involved:
- **AfriGIS**
- **Client / CLIENT NAME**

Summary of relationships:
1. AfriGIS and CLIENT NAME are parties to a confidentiality and non-disclosure agreement, highlighting a formal business relationship.
2. The contractual agreements define the obligations and rights of both AfriGIS and the Client, reinforcing the structured nature of their partnership.

Overall, these relationships underscore the importance of confidentiality and the formal obligations that govern the interactions between AfriGIS and its clients.
community_summary.1: The relationship between "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" and "RESTRICTIONS ON DISCLOSURE" highlights that confidential information is governed by specific restrictions aimed at preventing unauthorized sharing. These restrictions are crucial for safeguarding sensitive information, ensuring that it remains protected from disclosure without proper authorization. This relationship underscores the importance of compliance with disclosure restrictions to maintain confidentiality.

community_summary.6:
The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight the interconnectedness of various legal entities and their recognition under the law. 

1. **Natural Person and Juristic Person**: A natural person encompasses a juristic person, illustrating that legal rights and responsibilities are not limited to individuals but also extend to organizations.

2. **Partnership and Trust**: A partnership is classified as a type of juristic person, which can also encompass trusts. This indicates that both partnerships and trusts are recognized as legal entities with defined roles in business and legal contexts.

Overall, these relationships emphasize the broad scope of legal recognition, where both individuals and organizations (including partnerships and trusts) are acknowledged as entities capable of holding rights and responsibilities.
community_summary.7:
The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight the connections between various entities, primarily focusing on the concepts of time and corporate structure.

1. **Month and Year**: A month is a fundamental component of a year, with the year comprising 12 months. This relationship emphasizes the cyclical nature of time and how months collectively form a year.

2. **Company and Group of Companies**: A company can be part of a larger group of companies, which allows for shared resources and management. This indicates a collaborative corporate environment where individual companies operate under a unified structure.

3. **Employer and Group of Companies**: An employer may also be part of a group of companies, reflecting a corporate structure that encompasses multiple related entities. This relationship underscores the interconnectedness of employers within a broader corporate framework.

Overall, the summary illustrates the structural relationships between time (months and years) and corporate entities (companies and employers within groups), highlighting the significance of these connections in both temporal and organizational contexts.
community_summary.8: The relationship between "Business Day" and "Days" is defined as follows: a Business Day is a specific type of day that falls under the broader category of Days. This indicates that Business Days are a subset of Days, highlighting their significance in contexts such as commerce and finance, where certain activities are restricted to these designated days.
community_summary.9: The relationship between "Business Hours" and "Business Day" highlights that business hours are specifically defined for business days, indicating the operational hours within those days. This relationship emphasizes the significance of business hours as they are integral to the functioning of a business during the designated days of operation.
community_summary.10:
**Summary of Relationships:**

1. **Client and AfriGIS**: The Client is currently engaged in proprietary discussions with AfriGIS, indicating a collaborative or consultative relationship. This engagement is formalized through a contractual relationship where AfriGIS provides services to the Client.

2. **Contractual Agreement**: The relationship between the Client and AfriGIS is governed by a formal agreement, which outlines the services to be provided by AfriGIS.

3. **Exclusion of Third Parties**: The agreement explicitly distinguishes the roles and responsibilities of the Client and AfriGIS from any third parties, ensuring that only these two entities are involved in the contractual obligations.

4. **Client's Representation**: In the context of the agreement with AfriGIS, the Client represents the interests of the Client Brand, highlighting the importance of brand advocacy within the partnership.

Overall, the relationships emphasize a structured collaboration between the Client and AfriGIS, characterized by formal agreements, clear delineation of roles, and a focus on the Client Brand's interests.

community_summary.12:
**Summary of Relationships:**

1. **Receiving Party and Disclosing Party**: The Receiving Party acknowledges the proprietary nature and value of the Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party. This acknowledgment signifies the Receiving Party's recognition of the importance of the information received.

2. **Receiving Party's Role**: The Receiving Party is responsible for receiving, handling, and protecting the Confidential Information as stipulated in the agreement. This includes safeguarding the information against unauthorized disclosure and returning it upon request.

3. **Authorized Agent**: The Receiving Party acts as an authorized agent of the Disclosing Party, which entails a duty to manage the confidential information in accordance with the terms of the agreement.

4. **Indemnification**: The Receiving Party agrees to indemnify the Disclosing Party, protecting it from any losses or damages that may arise from unauthorized disclosures of the confidential information.

5. **Compliance and Advisory**: The Receiving Party is obligated to comply with requests from the Disclosing Party, including the destruction of materials. Additionally, the Receiving Party advises the Disclosing Party on necessary steps to safeguard its interests regarding the shared confidential information.

6. **Disclosure Relationship**: The relationship between the Receiving Party and the Disclosing Party is fundamentally based on the act of disclosing Confidential Information, where the Disclosing Party provides information under the terms of their agreement.

Overall, the relationships highlight the responsibilities and obligations of the Receiving Party in managing Confidential Information, emphasizing the importance of protection, compliance, and advisory roles in maintaining confidentiality and safeguarding the interests of the Disclosing Party.
community_summary.13: The relationship between "Confidential Information" and "Receiving Party" is characterized by the disclosure of sensitive information. This relationship is established under specific conditions outlined in an agreement, indicating that the Receiving Party is granted access to the Confidential Information. The nature of this relationship emphasizes the importance of confidentiality and the legal framework governing the sharing of such information.
community_summary.14: The relationships highlight that Johannesburg serves as a significant location for arbitration, specifically under the guidelines established by the Arbitration Foundation of Southern Africa. This indicates that any arbitration proceedings conducted in Johannesburg adhere to the rules set forth by this foundation, emphasizing the city's role as a central hub for arbitration activities in the region.
community_summary.15:
**Summary of Relationships:**

1. **Employee and Employer**: An employee is employed by an employer, which signifies a working relationship where the employee provides services in exchange for compensation. This relationship underscores the fundamental nature of employment, where the employer relies on the employee's skills and labor.

2. **Employee and Entity**: An employee may be associated with or employed by any entity, indicating that employment is not limited to a specific type of organization but can encompass various entities, including businesses, non-profits, and governmental organizations. 

Overall, these relationships highlight the essential dynamics of employment, emphasizing the reciprocal nature of the employee-employer relationship and the broad applicability of employment across different entities.

community_summary.20:
The relationships outlined involve a set of entities referred to as "Parties," which are engaged in a formal Agreement that governs their interactions and obligations. 

1. **Parties to Agreement**: The Parties are defined as the entities involved in the Agreement, which primarily focuses on confidentiality obligations.
   
2. **Engagement of Professional Advisors**: The Parties have agreed to disclose Confidential Information to professional advisors, indicating that these advisors are engaged to assist the Parties in fulfilling their responsibilities under the Agreement.

3. **Involvement in Agreement**: The Parties are actively involved in the Agreement, necessitating their cooperation to execute its terms and conditions effectively.

4. **Obligations and Responsibilities**: The Parties are bound by the Agreement, which clearly outlines their obligations and responsibilities towards one another.

5. **Dispute Resolution**: The Parties are required to meet to collaboratively resolve any disputes that may arise from the Agreement, emphasizing their commitment to amicable conflict resolution.

6. **Jurisdiction**: The Parties have consented to the jurisdiction of the North Gauteng Provincial Division of the High Court of the Republic of South Africa for any disputes stemming from the Agreement.

In summary, the Parties are interconnected through a formal Agreement that establishes their obligations, facilitates the engagement of professional advisors, mandates cooperation, and outlines procedures for dispute resolution, all under the jurisdiction of a specified court.

community_summary.22:
The relationships summarized below highlight the key aspects of an Agreement and its implications for the involved Parties and the Client Brand:

1. **Agreement Composition**: The Agreement is structured with various **Clauses** that detail specific terms and conditions, establishing a comprehensive legal framework.

2. **Rights and Obligations**: The Agreement confers specific **rights and obligations** on the **Party**, ensuring accountability and defining the legal responsibilities within the relationship.

3. **Definitions**: It includes definitions for relevant **Acronyms**, clarifying terminology used throughout the document.

4. **Legislative Reference**: The Agreement references the **Electronic Communications and Transactions Act**, indicating that communications required to be in writing can be sent electronically, thus relying on this legislation for validity.

5. **Commencement**: The **Signature Date** signifies the commencement of the Agreement's terms, marking when the obligations and rights become effective.

6. **Enforceability**: The Agreement is enforceable against the **Party**, meaning that the Party is legally bound to adhere to its terms.

7. **Client Brand Benefits**: Provisions within the Agreement are designed to benefit the **Client Brand**, allowing it to accept these terms as a third party.

8. **Governance**: The Agreement governs the actions and responsibilities of the **Parties**, establishing a framework for their interactions and obligations.

9. **Execution**: The Agreement is formally **signed by the Parties**, indicating their consent and commitment to the terms outlined.

Overall, the Agreement serves as a foundational document that delineates the rights, responsibilities, and legal frameworks governing the interactions between the Parties and the Client Brand, ensuring clarity and enforceability in their relationship.
community_summary.23: The relationship between "Governs" and "APPLICABLE LAW AND JURISDICTION" is centered on the resolution of "DISPUTES." The applicable law and jurisdiction clause establishes the legal framework that dictates how any disagreements will be addressed, ensuring clarity and structure in the dispute resolution process. This relationship underscores the importance of having a defined legal context for resolving conflicts.
community_summary.24:
The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight key aspects of contractual agreements and their implications:

1. **Warranties by Parties and Breach**: The warranties made by parties are crucial as they define the expectations and guarantees within a contract. A breach occurs when these warranties are violated, establishing a direct link between the warranties and breaches of contract.

2. **Signature Date and Client**: The 'Signature Date' is significant for the 'Client' as it signifies the formal acceptance of the agreement. This date marks the commencement of the Client's obligations under the contract, emphasizing its importance in the contractual timeline.

In summary, the relationships illustrate how warranties set the foundation for contractual obligations and breaches, while the Signature Date serves as a pivotal moment for the Client's responsibilities within the agreement.
community_summary.25:
The relationships outlined involve a Party and its interactions with an Agreement and Domicilium. 

1. **Party and Agreement**: Each Party is bound by the terms of the Agreement, which delineates their respective rights and obligations. This indicates that the Agreement is a crucial legal document that governs the relationship between the Parties.

2. **Party and Domicilium**: Each Party has a Domicilium, which serves as the designated location for receiving legal notices and communications. This emphasizes the importance of having a clear and recognized address for legal correspondence.

3. **Notification Requirement**: There is a mutual obligation for Parties to notify each other of any changes to their Domicilium. This requirement underscores the necessity for effective communication between Parties regarding their legal addresses, ensuring that all notices are properly received.

Overall, the relationships highlight the legal framework within which Parties operate, emphasizing their obligations under the Agreement and the importance of maintaining updated communication channels through their Domicilium.

community_summary.27:
The relationships outlined involve a Disclosing Party, Receiving Party, and AfriGIS, focusing on the handling of confidential information. 

1. **Disclosing Party and AfriGIS**: AfriGIS may act as a disclosing party in legal agreements, sharing confidential information.

2. **Disclosing Party and Receiving Party**: The Disclosing Party has a direct relationship with the Receiving Party, where it discloses and provides confidential information. This information is recognized as proprietary by the Receiving Party.

3. **Confidentiality Obligations**: The Receiving Party is required to destroy the confidential information upon request from the Disclosing Party, emphasizing the importance of safeguarding sensitive data.

4. **Third-Party Disclosure**: The Disclosing Party can share information with a third party only after obtaining prior written authorization, ensuring control over the dissemination of confidential information.

Overall, these relationships highlight the responsibilities and protocols surrounding the sharing of confidential information, underscoring the importance of legal agreements and the protection of proprietary data.
